summary: Re-scoped to aria2 large-file tail collapse fix (wrapper.py:1526). todo->doing.

Two-pass aria2 fix. Pass 1 (0.2.28 -s64/-k4M): bulk lifted to ~533MB/s live (Wan/LTX) — proved pipe is huge, NOT R2/NIC throttled — but every file still collapsed to ~6MB/s in the FINAL piece (-s caps COUNT so 17GB=270MB pieces, last piece = 1 connection). Pass 2 (0.2.29 -s128/-k1M): -k sets piece SIZE so final piece ~1MB, single-conn tail negligible. Published to R2 stable, served-file verified. Awaiting user re-test on Wan (17GB) — watch last 100MB.

DONE + user-accepted. wrapper 0.2.29 -s128/-k1M raised tail floor from 63KB/s crawl to steady ~11-14MB/s (live: LTX 57.8GB + Wan on fresh Pod). Accepted structural aria2 taper ceiling. NOT committed — other agent commits shared wrapper.py.
Archived completed card during board cleanup.
